project.name: PropManager SQLite to MySQL 9.4 Migration
project.description: Complete migration from SQLite to MySQL 9.4 including database-agnostic code refactoring, data migration, and performance optimization for production-ready multi-tenant operations.

rationale.description: SQLite is unsuitable for production multi-tenant SaaS applications. MySQL 9.4 provides concurrent access, better performance at scale, and production-grade reliability.
benefits: Concurrent read/write operations without file locking

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

code_compatibility.description: Fix SQLite-specific code to be database-agnostic (works on both SQLite and MySQL)

database_setup.description: Create MySQL database, run migrations, configure connection

data_migration.description: Export SQLite data and import to MySQL with validation

verification.description: Verify application works correctly on MySQL 9.4

items.title: Create Database-Agnostic Date Helper Trait
items.description: FinanceStatsService, DashboardService, and ReportService use SQLite's strftime() function which doesn't exist in MySQL. Create a trait with database-agnostic date extraction methods following the pattern already established in FinanceReportService::getDateDiffSql().
steps: Add getMonthSql(column) method: SQLite uses strftime('%m', col), MySQL uses MONTH(col)
Add getYearSql(column) method: SQLite uses strftime('%Y', col), MySQL uses YEAR(col)
Add getDateFormatSql(column, format) method for custom date formatting
Add getDateDiffSql(column, column2) method (move from FinanceReportService)
Add comprehensive unit tests for both database drivers
acceptance_criteria: Trait provides consistent API for date operations
Works correctly on both SQLite and MySQL
Unit tests pass on both database drivers
No direct strftime() or MySQL-specific date functions in application code
definition_of_done: Trait created with all date helper methods
Tests pass on both SQLite and MySQL
items.verification: Run unit tests: php artisan test --filter=DatabaseAgnosticQueriesTest

items.title: Refactor FinanceStatsService for MySQL Compatibility
items.description: FinanceStatsService has 12+ strftime() calls that will fail on MySQL. Refactor to use the DatabaseAgnosticQueries trait.
steps: Add use DatabaseAgnosticQueries trait to FinanceStatsService
Update all selectRaw() queries to use trait methods
Test on both SQLite and MySQL
acceptance_criteria: No strftime() calls remain in file
All statistical queries work on MySQL
Performance is acceptable (< 100ms for stats queries)
definition_of_done: All strftime() replaced with trait methods
items.verification: Load Finance Hub with MySQL; verify all stats display correctly

items.title: Refactor DashboardService for MySQL Compatibility
items.description: DashboardService uses strftime() for date-based calculations. Refactor to use database-agnostic queries.
steps: Add use DatabaseAgnosticQueries trait to DashboardService
Identify all strftime() calls in the file
Verify all widgets display correct data
items.verification: Load Dashboard with MySQL; verify all stats and charts display correctly

items.title: Refactor ReportService for MySQL Compatibility
items.description: ReportService may have SQLite-specific date functions. Audit and refactor as needed.
steps: Add use DatabaseAgnosticQueries trait if needed
Replace any strftime() or SQLite-specific functions
acceptance_criteria: All reports generate correctly on MySQL
items.verification: Generate all report types with MySQL; verify correct output

items.title: Audit and Fix All Raw SQL Queries
items.description: Search entire codebase for raw SQL that may be SQLite-specific. The exploration found 69 occurrences of selectRaw/whereRaw/DB::raw across 15 files.
steps: Run: grep -rn 'strftime' app/ to find all occurrences
Run: grep -rn 'JULIANDAY' app/ to find SQLite date functions
Run: grep -rn 'selectRaw\|whereRaw\|DB::raw' app/ to audit all raw SQL
Document each occurrence and whether it's database-agnostic
acceptance_criteria: Zero strftime() calls outside the trait
Zero JULIANDAY() calls outside the trait
All raw SQL is database-agnostic or uses the trait
definition_of_done: Grep returns zero results for SQLite-specific functions

items.title: Configure MySQL 9.4 Connection in Laravel
items.description: Set up MySQL 9.4 database and configure Laravel connection with optimal settings for production.
steps: Create MySQL database: CREATE DATABASE propmanager CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
Create MySQL user with appropriate privileges
Verify config/database.php has correct MySQL settings
Enable strict mode for data integrity

items.title: Run Migrations on MySQL 9.4
items.description: Run all Laravel migrations to create schema in MySQL. Fix any migration-specific issues.
steps: Fix any migration errors (ENUM syntax, default values, etc.)
acceptance_criteria: All migrations run successfully on MySQL
All indexes and foreign keys created
definition_of_done: php artisan migrate runs without errors
items.verification: php artisan migrate:status shows all migrations ran

items.title: Create Data Migration Script
items.description: Create artisan command to export SQLite data and import to MySQL with validation and error handling.
steps: Read all tables from SQLite in dependency order (respect foreign keys)
Disable foreign key checks during import
Add progress bars for user feedback
acceptance_criteria: Command migrates all data from SQLite to MySQL
definition_of_done: All tables have matching row counts
items.verification: Compare: SELECT COUNT(*) from each table in both databases

items.title: Backup SQLite Database
items.description: Create timestamped backup of SQLite database before migration.
acceptance_criteria: Backup file exists and is readable
Backup is complete copy of original

items.title: Run Test Suite on MySQL
items.description: Run full PHPUnit test suite against MySQL to verify all functionality works.
acceptance_criteria: All existing tests pass on MySQL
definition_of_done: Full test suite passes on MySQL
CI can run tests on both SQLite and MySQL
items.verification: php artisan test --env=mysql shows all tests passing

items.title: Manual Smoke Test on MySQL
items.description: Perform manual smoke testing of critical user flows on MySQL.
steps: Login as landlord, caretaker, and tenant
Test Dashboard loading and stats accuracy
Test Finance Hub - all tabs and reports

items.title: Performance Comparison Benchmark
items.description: Compare query performance between SQLite and MySQL to verify expected improvements.
steps: Benchmark Finance Hub load time on SQLite
Benchmark Finance Hub load time on MySQL
Benchmark Dashboard load time on both
Document results and any performance regressions
acceptance_criteria: MySQL performs equal or better than SQLite for all benchmarks
Concurrent access performs better on MySQL

executionOrder: MYS-005 - Audit and fix all raw SQL
MYS-008 - Migrate data from SQLite to MySQL
MYS-010 - Run test suite on MySQL

rollbackPlan.description: If migration fails, rollback is straightforward
steps: Restore SQLite backup if needed (use most recent backup): BACKUP=$(find database/backups -maxdepth 1 -name 'database_backup_*.sqlite' -type f -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -1 | cut -d' ' -f2-); [ -n "$BACKUP" ] && cp -- "$BACKUP" database/database.sqlite || echo 'ERROR: No backup files found in database/backups/'
Clear caches: php artisan config:clear && php artisan cache:clear
Application immediately works on SQLite again
notes: The restore command uses find with -printf for reliable sorting by modification time
The command handles filenames with spaces via proper quoting
If no backups exist, an error message is printed and no file is copied
Always verify backup existence before running: ls -la database/backups/

metrics: Zero strftime() or SQLite-specific SQL in app/ directory
All migrations run successfully on MySQL 9.4
All data migrated with matching row counts
Full test suite passes on MySQL
All critical user flows work correctly
MySQL performance equal or better than SQLite
